dslcRelationship.rule: DSLC establishes release-to-revenue readiness. SHIP consumes the DSLC decision as its prerequisite and never downgrades DSLC blockers.

evidence.postDslcBoundary: SHIP is the final release-management check after DSLC. It can block formal shipment even when DSLC passed, but it cannot override a DSLC blocker.

evidence.rule: QASC proof is consumed through DSLC and may also be referenced directly in the sealed proof pack. SHIP does not rescore QASC.

evidence.readyRule: DSLC is ready at 100/100 and every applicable SHIP control in every required pillar is satisfied or has an approved not-applicable determination.
evidence.notApplicableRule: A required control may be marked not-applicable only with rationale, named approver, and evidence of the applicability determination.
evidence.approvalRule: A Class A or Class S control marked satisfied is below benchmark unless it has evidence and a named approver. Agents may record the evidence but cannot synthesize the approval.
evidence.postDslcBoundary: SHIP is the final release-management check after DSLC. It can block formal shipment even when DSLC passed, but it cannot override a DSLC blocker.
evidence.claimBoundary: SHIP records legal, public-release, billing, and product-readiness approvals; an agent cannot synthesize a legal signature, revenue-recognition approval, public claim, or human release-manager attestation.
evidence.segregationOfDuties: The same actor must not both originate and sovereignly approve a legal instrument, public disclosure, pricing exception, revenue-recognition decision, or final release-manager attestation.

controls.evidence: SHIP runbook, rollout PRD, contract, schema, evaluator, and release manifest
